evidence: Build/check/test clean on merged main under the pinned toolchain after each of three shard merges and two fix-round merges (all six package suites zero-fail every round). scan-comment-citations reports totalInstances 0 repo-wide. The iterate-to-zero loop ran three adversarial audit rounds: round 1 over the merged shards confirmed semantic preservation across all nine vocabulary families and both directed truth-checks, and surfaced one laundered false claim (ops-registry header), one shard miss, two sibling ordinal families the enumeration had not covered (step-N, lowercase phase), gitignored-spec citations in pi-jit-agents, and emitted-string keeps inconsistent with the program's emitted-output standard -- all fixed; round 2 verified those fixes and found two false-claim comments the citation-inlining had introduced (a package-wide never-reads-.pi claim contradicted by the renderer registry's project tier; a one-dispatch-path claim contradicted by the pi-subprocess dispatch of acting agents) -- both restated to per-surface verified truth; round 3 verified every restated claim against the code (tier lists, phantom-tool binding, the two real executeAgent consumers, both subprocess dispatch sites) and passed with zero findings. Residual grep triage: every remaining pattern hit is a deliberate keep (scanner subject matter, fixture/payload data, self-defined in-file sequences, domain vocabulary).

criteria_results.criterion: Every OPAQUE-class site from the enumeration's nine families rewritten to plain English carrying the family's verified referent; no planning ordinals remain in packages/*/src non-test comments beyond the enumerated exclusions
scripts/ non-test comments carry no tracker-ID citations and no OPAQUE-class planning vocabulary; both facets covered; self-defined sequences excluded as locally-defined
Rewrites preserve each site's semantic content, stated as what the code does -- including truth-corrections where the old text or its inlined replacement contradicted current code
Comment-citation scanner reports zero instances repo-wide; build, check, and full test suite exit 0
